title: Governor Whitmer Signs Executive Directive to Explore Clean Energy Resources
content: Governor Gretchen Whitmer signed an executive directive creating an initiative to explore geologic hydrogen as a usable, clean fuel alternative. MichiganГўв‚¬в„ўs unique geology makes it one of the most promising regions in the world for geologic hydrogen, a naturally occurring clean energy resource. This new initiative will determine the possibility of tapping into MichiganГўв‚¬в„ўs geologic hydrogen and using this natural fuel to build the future of clean energy. Гўв‚¬Е“Michigan is a leader in clean-energy solutions,Гўв‚¬Вќ said Governor Whitmer. Гўв‚¬Е“This initiative will uncover more about MichiganГўв‚¬в„ўs geologic hydrogen stores, which have the potential to attract long-term investments in our state and create thousands of good paying jobs for Michiganders. Together, weГўв‚¬в„ўre building the future of clean energy in Michigan.Гўв‚¬Вќ Гўв‚¬Е“Geologic hydrogen represents a transformative opportunity for Michigan, a state historically rich in natural resources and innovation,Гўв‚¬Вќ said Jim Saber, President and CEO of NextEnergy. Гўв‚¬Е“By tapping into this abundant, naturally occurring resource, Michigan can lead the way in sustainable energy, create new jobs, and drive technological advancement. This isnГўв‚¬в„ўt just about energyГўв‚¬вЂќitГўв‚¬в„ўs about securing MichiganГўв‚¬в„ўs future as a leader in environmental stewardship and economic resilience, ensuring prosperity for generations to come.Гўв‚¬Вќ
author: Analysts Account
source: Office of The Governor of Michigan
